en.Horsted Keynes, Mid Sussex district, Wes: Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England, United Kingdom

nameMeanings.name: Sybilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sibilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Sybilla Lewknor was born in 1463, in Horsted Keynes, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Lewknor, was 68 and her mother, Johanna Halsham, was 41. She had at least 1 son with William Southe.

ko.lifeSummary: Sybilla Lewknor(이)가 1463년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 호르스테드 케인스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Lewknor(은)는 68세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Halsham(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 William Southe(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.
